meta.title: Electrician Website Design & Best-Practice Guidelines
meta.purpose: Authoritative ruleset for an AI coding assistant (Cursor) to follow when designing and building a Kansas City, KS electrician website. Derived from analysis of the top 15 KC-metro electrician sites. Rules are ordered by priority within each section. When two rules conflict, the one marked higher priority wins.
meta.context: Local home-services trade. Many visitors arrive mid-emergency on mobile and are vetting whether the business is real, local, licensed, and fairly priced. Speed-to-call and trust are the dominant conversion levers.
meta.howToUse: Treat every rule as a build requirement, not a suggestion. Before marking a page complete, run it against qualityChecklist. Do not invent business facts (license numbers, review counts, years in business); use the placeholders in businessProfile and flag them for the client to fill in.

businessProfile.instructions: Replace all PLACEHOLDER values with real client data. Never fabricate these.

recommendedStack.note: Flexible. Optimize for SEO, speed, and editability. If the client has an existing stack, adapt these rules to it.
recommendedStack.framework: Next.js (App Router) for static/SSG pages and fast first paint
recommendedStack.styling: Tailwind CSS with a centralized design-token config
recommendedStack.images: next/image with explicit width/height, AVIF/WebP, lazy-loading below the fold
recommendedStack.forms: Server action or API route with spam protection (honeypot + rate limit); no client-only forms that lose leads on JS failure
recommendedStack.analytics: Google Analytics 4 + call tracking; event on every CTA click and form submit

designPrinciples.rule: Lead with the call, not the brand.
designPrinciples.detail: The visitor's fastest path to converting is a phone call. The header must expose a tappable click-to-call number above the fold on every page, before any scroll, on both desktop and mobile.

designPrinciples.rule: Confront anxiety in the first viewport.
designPrinciples.detail: The hero must answer 'why you, why now' with a value proposition that addresses customer fear (safety, surprise pricing, trust). A van photo plus the company name alone is a failure.
License/insured status, review rating + count, years in business, and BBB (if applicable) must appear as visible designed badges high on the page, not buried in the footer.
Assume a stressed, one-handed mobile user. Thumb-reachable call button, large tap targets, no horizontal scroll, no tiny links.

designPrinciples.rule: One reserved accent color for action.
designPrinciples.detail: Pick a single high-contrast accent and use it ONLY for CTAs/buttons. Never color decorative elements with the action color.

designPrinciples.rule: Every service earns its own page.
designPrinciples.detail: Each service is a discrete, indexable page with real explanatory depth. This serves UX and SEO simultaneously.
The market gap: most competitors have either empathetic copy OR strong SEO architecture, rarely both. This site must do both.

colorPalette.note: Starting palette. Client may rebrand, but the ROLE of each color (especially accent reserved for CTAs) must be preserved.

primary.role: Brand / headers / navigation. Deep, trustworthy blue.

accent.role: RESERVED FOR CTAS ONLY. Phone buttons, request-service buttons, offers. Do not use decoratively.

colorPalette.contrastRule: All text must meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large). Accent color is for fills behind dark text or as a button with sufficient contrast; verify accent-on-white text passes before using it for text.

typography.headingFont: A strong, legible sans (e.g., Inter, Poppins, or Archivo). Self-host or use a fast font CDN with display=swap.
typography.bodyFont: A highly readable sans (e.g., Inter or system stack).
typography.rule: Body text never below 16px on mobile. Generous line-height for scannability.

spacing.rule: Use whitespace to separate sections; alternate white and neutralLight bands for rhythm.

ctaButton.appearance: Filled accent color, dark or white text passing contrast, bold, generous padding (min 48px height for tap target), rounded corners.
ctaButton.states: Distinct hover, focus-visible ring, and active states required.
ctaButton.rule: Primary CTA text is action-oriented: 'Call Now', 'Request Service', 'Get a Free Quote'. Never generic 'Submit' or 'Learn More' for the main action.

clickToCall.appearance: Phone icon + number, accent-colored, always tappable via tel: link.
clickToCall.placement: Pinned in header on all viewports; repeated in a sticky mobile footer bar.
clickToCall.rule: Wrap in <a href='tel:+1XXXXXXXXXX'>. Fire an analytics event on click.

trustBadgeRow.contents: Star rating + review count, 'Licensed & Insured', 'Years in business', BBB logo (if accredited), Google reviews logo.
trustBadgeRow.placement: Directly under hero and again near the primary form.

serviceCard.contents: Icon, service name, one-line benefit, link to the dedicated service page.
serviceCard.rule: Cards link to real pages, never to anchors on a single bloated services page.

testimonialCard.contents: Quote, customer first name + last initial, city, the specific job done, and the technician's name where available.
testimonialCard.rule: Named, job-specific testimonials only. No anonymous one-liners. Pull from real Google reviews; do not fabricate.

stickyMobileBar.placement: Fixed to bottom of viewport on mobile across all pages.

imagery.rule: Use authentic crew, truck, and completed-job photography. NO generic stock electrician imagery.
imagery.heroMedia: Optional short compressed hero video (muted, autoplay, loop) WITH a static image fallback that loads first. Video must not block first paint.
imagery.projectGallery: Include a gallery of real before/after work (panel upgrades, lighting, EV chargers). Each image has descriptive alt text.
imagery.altText: Every image needs descriptive, keyword-aware alt text (e.g., 'Electrician installing a 200-amp panel in a Kansas City, KS home').

globalNavigation.style: Services exposed as a mega-menu/dropdown listing each service page individually.
items: Home
Services (mega-menu)
Service Areas
About
Reviews
Blog
Contact
globalNavigation.rule: Do not hide services behind a single undifferentiated 'Services' link.

mustInclude: service-area block with embedded Google Map
top-of-funnel educational posts targeting informational queries

serviceServiceMatrix.instruction: Generate one dedicated, content-rich page per service. Each page must have UNIQUE, substantive copy (300+ words), not a thin template clone. Thin duplicated pages are a known SEO liability and are prohibited.
services: Electrical Panel Upgrades / Breaker Box Replacement
perServicePageMustInclude: Unique H1 with service + primary city
Explanatory educational content (e.g., why pre-1980 homes on 60-amp service need upgrades, NEC/GFCI context)
Relevant FAQ block with FAQ schema
Internal links to related services and to relevant city pages

locationPages.instruction: Generate a dedicated, indexable page per major service-area city, INCLUDING a dedicated 'Electrician in Kansas City, KS' page (primary target). Each must have locally tailored copy referencing neighborhoods, local context, or common local housing stock. Do NOT mass-produce thin near-duplicate city pages.
locationPages.rule: Quality over quantity. A handful of genuinely localized pages beats dozens of templated ones.

rules: Pin click-to-call in the header on every page and viewport.
Repeat a CTA after every major homepage section (visitors decide to act at unpredictable scroll depths).
Sticky bottom call+request bar on mobile.
Use action-oriented button copy; reserve the accent color for CTAs only.
Fire an analytics event on every CTA click and form submit.
Keep the primary request form short: Name, Phone, ZIP, short Message. Email optional.
Make a request form always reachable (off-canvas slide-out or sticky CTA that opens it) so visitors never navigate away to convert.
Include lead-segmentation field: 'I am a...' (Homeowner - small repair, Homeowner - installation, Property Manager, Contractor). One tap, helps routing.
Server-side handling with honeypot + rate limiting; never lose a lead to client JS failure.
Show a clear success state and a fallback phone number on error.
Minimize required fields; every extra field reduces completion.
Display review rating + count, licensed & insured, years in business, and BBB (if accredited) as designed badges high on the page.
Testimonials must name the customer, city, job, and technician where possible.
Show a project/photo gallery of real completed work as visual proof of competence.
Link out to the live Google Business Profile for verifiability.

pricingTransparency.rule: Turn pricing reassurance into a visible designed promise block. Examples of patterns proven in this market: 'No-surprises, charge-by-the-job pricing', 'Flat-rate pricing - know the cost before we start', 'Free estimates'. Use only the claims the client can truthfully make.

offersAndLeadMagnets.rule: Provide at least one lower-commitment entry point styled as a distinct card: a priced safety inspection, a downloadable home electrical safety checklist, a dollar-off coupon, or military/senior discount. Use real, client-approved offers only.

rules: Set a self-referencing canonical on every page.
Unique title tag and meta description per page; include primary service + city and the phone number in the description.
Set robots to index,follow on public pages; include max-image-preview:large.
Add Open Graph and Twitter card tags with a branded share image.
Generate sitemap.xml and robots.txt.
Use semantic HTML5 landmarks (header, nav, main, footer, section) and one H1 per page.
All internal links use descriptive anchor text; build a logical internal-linking mesh between services and city pages.

schemas: LocalBusiness / Electrician (name, address, geo, hours, phone, priceRange, areaServed, sameAs to social + GBP)
Service schema on each service page
FAQPage schema on pages with FAQ blocks
AggregateRating / Review schema fed by real reviews
structuredData.rule: Schema must reflect only true, on-page information. Do not mark up fake reviews or ratings.

rules: Each service and city page has unique, substantive copy - no thin templated duplicates.
Target informational queries with a blog (e.g., 'signs you need a panel upgrade', 'which home appliances use the most electricity', 'is your wiring up to code').
Naturally include 'Kansas City, KS', 'Wyandotte County', and metro suburb names where truthful and relevant - never keyword-stuff.
Embed a Google Map and list the service-area suburbs as a designed block.
Ensure NAP (Name, Address, Phone) is identical site-wide and matches the Google Business Profile exactly.
Build a dedicated Kansas City, KS landing page as the primary local target.
Encourage review generation; surface fresh named reviews on-site.
Add LocalBusiness schema with accurate geo-coordinates and areaServed.
Design and test mobile-first; verify the entire call/request flow one-handed.
Tap targets minimum 48x48px with adequate spacing.
Sticky bottom bar with Call + Request Service on all mobile pages.
No horizontal scrolling; fluid type via clamp().
Defer/lazy-load below-the-fold media so the hero and phone number paint fast.
Optimize and serve images as AVIF/WebP with explicit dimensions to prevent layout shift.
Hero video must be compressed, lazy, and have an image fallback that paints first; never block first contentful paint.
Limit third-party scripts; load analytics/chat asynchronously.
Preload critical fonts with display=swap; avoid render-blocking CSS/JS.
Static-generate (SSG) marketing pages where possible.
Slow load disproportionately hurts mobile/emergency visitors - treat performance as a conversion feature, not a nicety.
Meet WCAG 2.1 AA: contrast, focus-visible states, keyboard navigability.
All images have descriptive alt text; decorative images use empty alt.
Forms have associated labels and accessible error messaging.
tel: and mailto: links are real, keyboard-accessible anchors.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion: disable autoplay video/animation when set.

copyGuidelines.voice: Trustworthy, plain-spoken, locally rooted. Speak to the homeowner's anxiety about hidden wiring, safety, and surprise costs.
rules: Lead with empathy and reassurance, then capability - not a feature dump.
Explain the 'why' (educate), the way the strongest competitors do, to build authority.
Use concrete operational facts (founding year, fleet size, hours, warranty terms) instead of vague superlatives.
Every page ends with a clear next step (call or request service).
Do not fabricate credentials, review counts, awards, or years in business.

doNot: Do not bury the phone number below the fold or in the footer only.
Do not use generic stock electrician photography.
Do not put all services on one bloated page with anchor links.
Do not mass-produce thin, near-duplicate city or service pages.
Do not run the business on two near-identical domains (duplicate-content dilution).
Do not use the accent/action color for decorative elements.
Do not use anonymous, generic testimonials.
Do not let a hero video block first paint or ship without an image fallback.
Do not fabricate any business fact, review, rating, license, or award.
Do not use a client-only form that can silently drop a lead.
Do not use generic CTA copy like 'Submit' for the primary action.

qualityChecklist.instruction: Every page must pass before it is considered complete.
items: Click-to-call visible and tappable above the fold (header + sticky mobile bar).
Hero communicates value proposition + has a primary CTA.
Trust badges (rating, licensed/insured, years) visible high on the page.
Primary request form is short, segmented, server-handled, and always reachable.
Pricing-transparency promise present where relevant.
At least one lead magnet/offer present.
Named, job-specific testimonials present.
Service-area block with embedded Google Map.
Unique title, meta description (with phone), canonical, OG/Twitter tags.
Appropriate JSON-LD schema implemented and accurate.
One H1, semantic landmarks, descriptive alt text on all images.
Mobile flow tested one-handed; tap targets >= 48px.
LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, no render-blocking hero video.
WCAG AA contrast and focus states verified.
No fabricated facts; all PLACEHOLDER values replaced or flagged.
